stmt: For atomic goals, successful runs of `proveAux` are in height-preserving correspondence with focused decomposition trees: `proveAux n Γ (Tp.atom s) = true` exactly when there exists a focused atomic decomposition tree for `Γ ⇒ Tp.atom s` of height at most `n`.
summary_delta: This turns the present existential bound into an exact preservation theorem between executable proof search and structural focused objects. It would supply the missing interface between the current Boolean procedure and the theory-level decomposition trees that the frontier now needs.
priority_rationale: This is the clearest summary-level bridge from bounded `proveAux` success to exact focused objects, and it would make atomic proof search structurally canonical rather than merely height-bounded.

stmt: ∀ {Γ : List Tp} {A : Tp}, (∀ x ∈ Γ, is_atom x) → Γ ⇒ A → ∃ s : String, Γ = [Tp.atom s]
summary_delta: The verified refutation shows that the original failure comes from the conclusion side, not from the size of an all-atomic antecedent. This would salvage the true rigidity boundary for atomic inputs and directly advances the current goal of exact characterization for small contexts.
priority_rationale: This would salvage a genuine surviving boundary from the refuted all-atomic theorem, but it gives a context criterion only and not yet the full canonical derivation picture.

stmt: If (∀ x ∈ Γ, is_atom x) and Γ ⇒ A, then for some s : String one has Γ = [Tp.atom s], and the derivation of [Tp.atom s] ⇒ A can be put into a form built from the axiom by slash-right introductions only.
summary_delta: This would convert the counterexample pattern into an explicit normal-form theorem for proof search on atomic data. It supplies the missing bridge from the primitive sequent rules to a reusable decomposition principle, which is exactly the frontier the current theory state says is missing.
priority_rationale: This turns the atomic-context counterexample into a positive normal-form theorem, but it is still narrower than the general recursive search and focusing interface.

stmt: For the right-only fragment, give an exact iterated classification of atomic conclusions: for every context Γ and atom s, Γ ⇒ Tp.atom s iff there exist n >= 0, types A_1, ..., A_n, and blocks Δ_1, ..., Δ_n such that Γ = [(((Tp.atom s ⧸ A_n) ⧸ ...) ⧸ A_1)] ++ Δ_1 ++ ... ++ Δ_n and each Δ_i ⇒ A_i.
summary_delta: This is the full recursive version of the solved one-step decomposition in the pure right fragment. It would turn local last-rule analysis into a reusable head-normal-form theorem and directly connect the active proof theory to the broader grammar-style structural picture highlighted in the materials.
priority_rationale: An exact right-fragment head-normal-form theorem would provide a reusable fragment taxonomy with grammar-facing relevance, though it remains fragment-specific.

stmt: Give the mirror-image exact classification for the left-only fragment: for every context Γ and atom s, Γ ⇒ Tp.atom s iff there exist n >= 0, types A_1, ..., A_n, and blocks Δ_1, ..., Δ_n such that Γ = Δ_n ++ ... ++ Δ_1 ++ [A_1 ⧹ (A_2 ⧹ ... (A_n ⧹ Tp.atom s) ...)] and each Δ_i ⇒ A_i.
summary_delta: This would isolate the left-handed analogue of the right-fragment head normal form, showing that the one-step atomic decomposition scales to a sharp structural criterion rather than staying a local proof-script fact. It also advances the fragment taxonomy inside the current theory instead of adding another nearby slash lemma.
priority_rationale: The left-fragment mirror would sharpen the one-sided fragment taxonomy, but its impact is still below the general focused-search correspondence problems.

stmt: For every context `Γ`, the list `candidates Γ` is an exact classification of the possible last principal steps of derivations with atomic succedent: a derivation of `Γ ⇒ Tp.atom s` realizes one of those candidates exactly when the two recursive subgoals attached to that candidate are derivable.
summary_delta: This isolates the proof-search boundary in a sharp way: not just that some outer decomposition exists, but that the algorithmic candidate enumeration is precisely the proof-theoretic space of last steps. That is a stronger and more reusable bridge from sequent rules to recursive search.
priority_rationale: This is an important exactness upgrade for atomic proof search, but it still captures only the last-step interface rather than the full recursive correspondence.
